calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Blackman for Annie Peterson (Decedent's Widow). Greer Smith for and with Willie Gardner (Executor).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of fees incurred with having Annie removed from the property and whether said fees should be paid by the Estate. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harrison.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-31-2011 Continued To Jun-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-23-2011 Continued To May-31-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-02-2011 Continued To May-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Blackman for Annie Peterson. Greer Smith for Willie Gardner (Executor).

The Court stated that the matter before it today is the petition for instructions for the sale of the real property. Mr. Blackman stated that the move is currently taking place and that Ms. Peterson is on her way to Georgia. Ms. Smith stated that the purpose of today's hearing was to address the issue of fees. The Court stated that the notice regarding fees was less than fifteen days. Mr. Blackman stated that proper notice was not given but that he wanted the matter heard today. Ms. Smith presented argument in support of a grant of her request noting that she was simply seeking an order stating that Ms. Peterson would be responsible for the fees; the fees would come out of her money. In addition, Ms. Smith stated that the amount of the fees will be addressed at the time of the distribution and that this is the way that she has always done it. Mr. Blackman presented argument in opposition to the fees requested stating that Ms. Peterson's husband left her in a precarious situation. The Court noted that the sale was going forward. Ms. Smith stated that they asked for Ms. Peterson's cooperation in trying to see the house, that they didn't ask her to move, and that they had sent her two letters. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of The Stipulation
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for Willie Gardner (Administrator).

Ms. Greer provided the original stipulation and order (which was signed in open Court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Continued To Sep-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Blackman for and with Annie Peterson. Greer Smith for and with Willie Gardner (Executor).

Mr. Blackman stated that he filed an opposition yesterday, that his Client had a medical issue, that his Client lived in the house for 38 years, that she was frightened, that she was going to have surgery, that he could not commit to a strict move out date due to her health problems and that he filed the petition because of no response to his phone calls and letters. At the suggestion of the Court, the Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss the matter. Upon returning, Counsel reported that they settled the matter. The terms of the settlement were stated for the record. The property is to be vacated by September 6, 2010. Ms. Peterson may file an ex parte petition for an extension in the event of an emergency. If Ms. Peterson does not cooperate with showing of the property, the Executor may apply for an expedited vacate date. Ms. Peterson agreed to showing of the property to prospective purchasers with a 24 hour notice and to Broker's tours with 72 hours notice after July 21, 2010 and immediate placing a "for sale" sign on the property. Status of filing of the stipulation which is to be prepared by Ms. Greer was set for July 13, 2010. The Petition for Instructions was continued to September 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 along with the issue regarding Attorney's fees.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
